name: ConfigCat Feature Flag API
what_it_does: Feature flag and configuration management service with REST API and 25+ SDK support. Provides targeting rules, percentage rollouts, and per-user flag evaluation without server-side calls.
use_cases: Toggling features on/off programmatically for deployment automation agents
Managing percentage rollouts for gradual feature releases via Management API
Targeting specific user segments with different feature configurations
Coordinating feature flags across microservices in multi-repo deployments
Auditing flag change history and rollback automation
not_for: A/B testing with statistical significance analysis (use Optimizely or GrowthBook)
Real-time configuration that needs sub-millisecond latency
best_when: An agent needs to manage feature flags across multiple services and languages with minimal SDKs, or needs to automate release gates in CI/CD pipelines.
avoid_when: You need full experimentation with statistical analysis — use LaunchDarkly or Optimizely.

auth.notes: Management API uses Basic Auth with Public API credentials. SDK config fetching uses SDK Key (read-only per environment). Public API credentials from ConfigCat dashboard with read/write access to configs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 10M feature flag requests/month, 1 product, 2 configs, 2 environments
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $39/month; Smart from $79/month; Enterprise from $399/month
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development and small production. Per-month pricing based on MAU (monthly active users) and feature flag evaluations. No per-seat pricing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published. Management API well-documented with examples. SDK evaluation is local (no network call for each flag check). Webhook for config changes enables reactive agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flag creation and updates are idempotent by name. Setting flag value is deterministic. Management API operations are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate auth methods: SDK key for flag reads vs Basic Auth for management
SDKs cache config locally — management changes may take up to 60s to propagate without webhook
Config products/environments/flags hierarchy must be navigated to address correct resource
Flag evaluation happens client-side via SDK — Management API for CRUD, SDK for reads

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II and ISO 27001. GDPR-compliant with EU data residency. TLS enforced. SDK keys are read-only — Management API keys separate. SDK evaluation is local, reducing data exposure.
